name: Operation Cobalt Kitty: The Feline Intrusion
description:
Background

The Security Operations Center (SOC) at our organization has detected a series of alarming activities. Initial intelligence and threat hunting efforts suggest a highly sophisticated Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) group has compromised our network. The observed Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures (TTPs) strongly resemble the notorious Operation Cobalt Kitty.

The Incident

The breach began when an employee received a highly targeted spearphishing email with a malicious attachment (CV.doc). Since then, our SIEM has ingested a flurry of alerts. The threat actors have deployed a robust arsenal of advanced techniques, including:

  • Persistence mechanisms via suspicious scheduled tasks (using mshta.exe) and malicious registry run keys.
  • Defense evasion using obfuscated PowerShell scripts and executing payloads hidden within NTFS Alternate Data Streams (ADS).
  • In-memory execution of Cobalt Strike beacons.
  • Covert Command and Control (C2) communications, including DNS tunneling and customized Outlook macro backdoors.
  • Credential dumping and subsequent lateral movement using Pass-the-Hash and WMI execution.

Your Mission

As the Lead Incident Responder, you are provided with a complete export of the SIEM alerts generated during the attack. Your task is to sift through the noise, identify the true positives, and piece together the entire attack kill chain.

Can you connect the dots, track the adversary's lateral movement, and uncover the full extent of the compromise? Good luck, hunter!
